# Facebook Post — OpenAI Partners with Accenture, BCG, McKinsey, and Capgemini in Enterprise Push
# Week: Week of 2026-02-23
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-03-02T18:47:02.345Z

---

The way enterprise AI gets sold just fundamentally changed.

OpenAI announced multiyear partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey this week. These consulting giants will now help OpenAI's enterprise customers define and implement their AI strategies. OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar noted that enterprise accounts for roughly 40% of revenue today, with a target of 50% by year-end. Anthropic is making parallel moves with Deloitte and Accenture.

Here's the executive takeaway: when model providers partner with the firms that already sit in your strategy sessions, AI stops being a technology purchase and becomes a business transformation engagement. The consultants framing your AI roadmap will increasingly arrive with a preferred vendor already in mind.

That's not inherently bad — but it demands awareness. The companies that will extract the most value from AI are those building strategies around their own business objectives, not around a single provider's ecosystem.

Vendor-neutral AI strategy isn't a nice-to-have anymore. It's a competitive requirement.

GYanTech AI helps executive teams build AI roadmaps designed for their business — not a vendor's growth plan -> www.gyantechai.com

#EnterpriseAI #AIStrategy #OpenAI #BusinessTransformation

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI entered multiyear partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey & Co. — CNBC, Feb 23, 2026
- OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar stated enterprises account for roughly 40% of OpenAI's business, with expectations to reach ~50% by end of year — CNBC, Feb 23, 2026
- Anthropic has also inked deals with consulting giants including Deloitte and Accenture — TechCrunch, Feb 23, 2026
